summary: Malicious npm package exfiltrates console output and images to attacker-controlled Telegram/Firebase
details: Package 0e@0.0.109 contains unambiguous supply-chain attack code. On require, index.js replaces the global console with a Proxy that POSTs every console.log/info/warn/error/dir call to a hardcoded Telegram bot (token 1998266306:AAE..., chat_ids -1001482347974, -414529982, 465081843, -1001476159429) and PUTs payloads to https://iiilll.firebaseio.com/. It also ships a hardcoded imgbb API key (af7cad64d90d19e2a26889f92f6b3ed8) to upload arbitrary image data routed through the same Firebase relay. Simply requiring the module persistently hijacks the process-wide console for any dependent code. Static YARA rule a static pattern match independently confirms the Telegram exfil endpoints in index.js:81 and src/6cc.js:31. Three independent signals — hardcoded attacker-controlled Telegram exfil infra, hardcoded third-party API keys (matches credential-distribution precedent), and global console hijack at import time — for a package self-described as a 'Fetch API helper' that has no legitimate need for any of these behaviors.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
